title: haloragis aspera
intro: Haloragis aspera, also known as rough-rush or prickly water dock, is a flowering plant native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Haloragis aspera include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
